document.title: FMEA Table
document.documentType: Risk Management Report attachment / FMEA table

sourceSummary.oneLine: FMEA table for the ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector according to ISO 14971.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports risk management by identifying hazards, hazardous situations, harms, risk controls, verification or validation evidence, and residual risk acceptability.
keyPoints: Document identifies product name as Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and model name as ADD.
Risk items include electrical energy, electromagnetic energy, mechanical energy, heat energy, software error, battery, labeling, usability, and biocompatibility hazards.
Risk controls reference IEC 60601-1 test reports, EMC test report, S/W validation report, usability engineering report, bio-compatibility test report, labels, and user manual.
Listed residual risk evaluations are generally marked Acceptable after risk control.

evidence.claim: The document is titled FMEA Table.
The FMEA table is according to ISO 14971.
The product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The 2021.04.02 revision added the coversheet and revised the product name.
evidence.evidenceText: Add the Coversheet, Revise the product name 2021.04.02

evidence.claim: Prepared, reviewed, and approved signatures are dated 2021.04.02.
The EMC-related risk control states the product is designed to meet IEC 60601-1-2.
evidence.evidenceText: It is designed to meet IEC 60601-1-2.

evidence.claim: Safety signs and labeling are designed to meet ISO 7010.
evidence.evidenceText: Labels are designed to meet international standards ISO 7010

evidence.claim: The device has a rechargeable battery.
The ADD has a Type B applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD has the type B applied part

evidence.claim: Software risks are verified using an S/W Validation Report.
Usability risks reference IEC 62366 and usability engineering reports.
The overall residual risk after controls is repeatedly evaluated as acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk After Control ... Acceptable ... Overall residual risk evaluation ... Acceptable

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is only present in source metadata as authoritySignal MFDS/domestic and is not stated in the extracted source text.
Product metadata references HAD/A/F 1417-1717 and source path references ADD(HAD1417MCW), while the source text states model name ADD.
Manufacturer name is not present in the source text.
OCR/text quality issues are present, including misspellings and corrupted cell text such as Mechnical, enlosure, fornt, PMES, and N+H38:AB38E38I38:AB38.
